topic: India's Smartphone Market Faces Challenges Due to AI-Driven Memory Crunch
headline: SMARTPHONE SALES CRASH DUE TO AI-DRIVEN MEMORY CRUNCH, PRICES SOAR BY 30% IN JUST 6 MONTHS
summary: Smartphone sales, particularly budget models, are slowing due to surging random access memory (RAM) prices. The cost of memory now accounts for over 40% of the total cost of a low-end phone. Phone manufacturers like Samsung and Oppo are increasing prices, and others are expected to follow. This trend may also impact other electronic devices such as smart TVs and medical devices.

summary_sources.title: How consumers are paying for the AI boom
AI vs smartphones: How a global RAM shortage may drive up phone prices
BT warns of smartphone price rises due to chip shortages from AI boom | BT | The Guardian
Smartphones and PCs may soon cost up to 20% more as AI fuels memory shortage

sections.paragraph: The surge in artificial intelligence (AI) has led to a significant increase in the cost of random access memory (RAM), a crucial component in smartphones and computers. As a result, the cost of memory as a percentage of the total cost of a low-end phone has risen from under 10% to over 40%. This increase in memory costs is expected to be passed on to consumers, with price hikes of $150 to $200 predicted. The shortage of memory chips is mainly driven by tech firms buying up large quantities to power datacentres relied on by AI, which could lead to shortages and price increases for smartphones and other electronic devices. According to Gartner, Inc., worldwide PC shipments are projected to decline 10.4% and smartphone shipments to drop by 8.4% in 2026, with PC prices expected to increase by 17% and smartphone prices by 13% compared to 2025 levels.

sources.title: More expensive phones, PCs: How consumers are paying for the AI boom
BT warns of smartphone price rises due to chip shortages from AI boom | BT | The Guardian
AI vs smartphones: How a global RAM shortage may drive up phone prices

sections.paragraph: The prices of smartphones are increasing in India, with brands such as OnePlus and Nothing raising prices across several models due to a global RAM shortage. The price hike is attributed to the surging cost of random access memory, which has more than tripled in value since October 2025. This surge in memory cost is largely driven by the explosive growth in data centers that power artificial intelligence, which has shifted its reliance from graphics processing units to RAM. As a result, the cost of memory now accounts for over 40% of the total cost of a low-end phone, up from under 10% previously. This increase in memory costs is expected to be passed on to consumers, with estimated price hikes of $150 to $200, and may lead to a decline in worldwide PC and smartphone shipments, with Gartner predicting a 10.4% and 8.4% drop in 2026, respectively.
